{\n579: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n580: \tif err != nil {\n581: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n582: \t}\n583: \tstart, err := \t}\n594: \n595: \tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n596: \tif err != nil {\n597: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n598: \t}\n599: \n600: \tif step <= 0 {\n601: \t\treturn Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n602: \t}\n603: \n604: \t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n605: \t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n606: \tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n607: \t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n608: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n609: \t}\n610: \n611: \tctx := r.Context()\n612: \tif to := promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n715: \tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n716: \tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n717: \tswitch {\n718: \tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n719: \t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n720: \tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n721: \t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n722: \tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n723: \t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n724: \t}\n725: \n726: \tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n727: \t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n728: \t}\n729: \n730: \treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n731: }\n732: \n733: func (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n734: \tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n735: \tif err != nil {\n736: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n737: \t}\n738: \n739: \tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n740: \tif err != nil {\n741: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n742: \t}\n743: \tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n744: \tif err != nil {\n745: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n746: \t}\n747: \n748: \tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n749: \tif err != nil {\n750: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n751: \t}\n752: \n753: \thints := &storage.LabelHints{\n754: \t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n755: \t}\n756: \n757: \tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n758: \tif err != nil {\n759: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n760: \t}\n761: \tdefer q.Close()\n762: \n763: \tvar (\n764: \t\tnames    []string\n765: \t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n766: \t)\n767: \tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n768: \t\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n769: \n770: \t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n771: \t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err := q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n772: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n773: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, nil}\n774: \t\t\t}\n775: \n776: \t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n777: \t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n778: \t\t\t\tlabelNamesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n779: \t\t\t}\n780: \t\t}\n781: \n782: \t\t// Convert the map to an array.\n783: \t\tnames = make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\n784: \t\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\n785: \t\t\tnames = append(names, key)\n786: \t\t}\n787: \t\tslices.Sort(names)\n788: \t} else {\n789: \t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n790: \t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n791: \t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n792: \t\t}\n793: \t\tnames, warnings, err = q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n794: \t\tif err != nil {\n795: \t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, GlobalURLOptions struct {\n1069: \tListenAddress string\n1070: \tHost          string\n1071: \tScheme        string\n1072: }\n1073: \n1074: // sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n1075: // Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n1076: // original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n1077: // brackets.\n1078: func sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n1079: \thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n1080: \tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n1081: \t\tvar errWithPort error\n1082: \t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n1083: \t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n1084: \t\t\terr = nil\n1085: \t\t}\n1086: \t}\n1087: \treturn host, port, err\n1088: }\n1089: \n1090: func getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n1091: \thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n1092: \tif err != nil {\n1093: \t\treturn u, err\n1094: \t}\n1095: \n1096: \tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n1097: \t\tif host == lhr {\n1098: \t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n1099: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1100: \t\t\t\treturn u, err\n1101: \t\t\t}\n1102: \n1103: \t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n1104: \t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n1105: \t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n1106: \t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n1107: \t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n1108: \t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n1109: \t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n1110: \t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n1111: \t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n1112: \t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n1113: \t\t\t} else {\n1114: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n1115: \t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n1116: \t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n1117: \t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n1118: \t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n1119: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1120: \t\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n1121: \t\t\t\t}\n1122: \t\t\t\tu.Host = host\n1123: \t\t\t\tif port != \"\" {\n1124: \t\t\t\t\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\n1125: \t\t\t\t}\n1126: \t\t\t}\n1127: \t\t\tbreak\n1128: \t\t}\n1129: \t}\n1130: \n1131: \treturn u, nil\n1132: }\n1133: \n1134: func (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1135: \tnames \t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n1430: \t\t\t}\n1431: \t\t}\n1432: \t}\n1433: \n1434: \t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n1435: \tres := nil}\n1449: }\n1450: \n1451: // RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\n1452: type RuleDiscovery struct {\n1453: \tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n1454: \tGroupNextToken string {\n1504: \tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1505: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1506: \t}\n1507: \n1508: \t\t}\n1513: \t\treturn set\n1514: \t}\n1515: \n1516: \trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n1517: \trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n1518: \tfSet := typ), \"type\")\n1531: \t}\n1532: \n1533: \treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n1534: \treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n1535: \n1536: \texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n1537: \tif err != nil {\n1538: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n1539: \t}\n1540: \n1541: \tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := false\n1549: \n1550: \tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n1551: \t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n1552: \t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n1553: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554: \t\t\t}\n1555: \t\t\tfoundToken = true\n1556: \t\t}\n1557: \n1558: \t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n1559: \t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n1560: \t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n1581: \t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n1582: \n1583: \t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n1584: \t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n1585: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1586: \t\t\t\t}\n1587: \t\t\t}\n1588: \n1589: \t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n1590: \t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n1591: \t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n1592: \t\t\t}\n1593: \t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n1594: \t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n1595: \t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n1596: \t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1597: \t\t\t\t}\n1598: \t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts nil}\n1636: \t\t\t}\n1637: \n1638: \t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n1639: \t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n1640: \t\t\t}\n1641: \t\t}\n1642: \n1643: \t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n1644: \t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n1645: \t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n1646: \t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n1647: \t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n1657: \t}\n1658: \n1659: \tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n1660: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1661: }\n1662: \n1663: func parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n1664: \texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n1665: \n1666: \tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n1667: \treturn -1, \"\", nil\n1707: }\n1708: \n1709: func getRuleGroupNextToken(file, group string) string {\n1710: \th := sha1.New()\n1711: \th.Write([]byte(file + \";\" + group))\n1712: \treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n1713: }\n1714: \n1715: type prometheusConfig struct {\n1716: \tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n1717: }\n1718: \n1719: func (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MyGi1bSAUUK7NPiQ56qzde","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: ---\\n2: title: HTTP API\\n3: sort_rank: 7\\n4: ---\\n5: \\n6: The current stable HTTP API is reachable under `/api/v1` on a Prometheus\\n7: server. Any non-breaking additions will be added under that endpoint.\\n8: \\n9: ## Format overview\\n10: \\n11: The API response format is JSON. Every successful API request returns a `2xx`\\n12: status code.\\n13: \\n14: Invalid requests that reach the API handlers return a JSON error object\\n15: and one of the following HTTP response codes:\\n16: \\n17: - `400 Bad Request` when parameters are missing or incorrect.\\n18: - `422 Unprocessable Entity` when an expression can't be executed\\n19:   ([RFC4918](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4918#page-78)).\\n20: - `503 Service Unavailable` when queries time out or abort.\\n21: \\n22: Other non-`2xx` codes may be returned for errors occurring before the API\\n23: endpoint is reached.\\n24: \\n25: An array of warnings may be returned if there are errors that do\\n26: not inhibit the request execution. An additional array of info-level\\n27: annotations may be returned for potential query issues that may or may\\n28: not be false positives. All of the data that was successfully collected\\n29: will be returned in the data field.\\n30: \\n31: The JSON response envelope format is as follows:\\n32: \\n33: ```json\\n34: {\\n35:   \\\"status\\\": \\\"success\\\" | \\\"error\\\",\\n36:   \\\"data\\\": <data>,\\n37: \\n38:   // Only set if status is \\\"error\\\". The data field may still hold\\n39:   // additional data.\\n40:   \\\"errorType\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n41:   \\\"error\\\": \\\"<string>\\\",\\n42: \\n43:   // Only set if there were warnings while executing the request.\\n44:   // There will still be data in the data field.\\n45:   \\\"warnings\\\": [\\\"<string>\\\"],\\n46:   // Only set if there were info-level annotations while executing the request.\\n47:   \\\"infos\\\": [\\\"<string>\\\"]\\n48: }\\n49: ```\\n50: \\n51: Generic placeholders are defined as follows:\\n52: \\n53: * `<rfc3339 | unix_timestamp>`: Input timestamps may be provided either in\\n54: [RFC3339](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3339.txt) format or as a Unix timestamp\\n55: in seconds, with optional decimal places for sub-second precision. Output\\n56: timestamps are always represented as Unix timestamps in seconds.\\n57: * `<series_selector>`: Prometheus [time series\\n58: selectors](basics.md#time-series-selectors) like `http_requests_total` or\\n59: `http_requests_total{method=~\\\"(GET|POST)\\\"}` and need to be URL-encoded.\\n60: * `<duration>`: [the subset of Prometheus float literals using time units](basics.md#float-literals-and-time-durations).\\n61: For example, `5m` refers to a duration of 5 minutes.\\n62: * `<bool>`: boolean values (strings `true` and `false`).\\n63: \\n64: Note: Names of query parameters that may be repeated end with `[]`.\\n65: \\n66: ## Expression queries\\n67: \\n68: Query language expressions may be evaluated at a single instant or over a range\\n69: of time. The sections below describe the API endpoints for each type of\\n70: expression query.\\n71: \\n72: ### Instant queries\\n73: \\n74: The following endpoint evaluates an instant query at a single point in time:\\n75: \\n76: ```\\n77: GET /api/v1/query\\n78: POST /api/v1/query\\n79: ```\\n80: \\n81: URL query parameters:\\n82: \\n83: - `query=<string>`: Prometheus expression query string.\\n84: - `time=<rfc3339 | unix_timestamp>`: Evaluation timestamp. Optional.\\n85: - `timeout=<duration>`: Evaluation timeout. Optional. Defaults to and\\n86:    is capped by the value of the `-query.timeout` flag.\\n87: - `limit=<number>`: Maximum number of returned series. Doesn’t affect scalars or strings but truncates the number of series for matrices and vectors. Optional. 0 means disabled.\\n88: \\n89: The current server time is used if the `time` parameter is omitted.\\n90: \\n91: You can URL-encode these parameters directly in the request body by using the `POST` method and\\n92: `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header. This is useful when specifying a large\\n93: query that may breach server-side URL character limits.\\n94: \\n95: The `data` section of the query result has the following format:\\n96: \\n97: ```json\\n98: {\\n99:   \\\"resultType\\\": \\\"matrix\\\" | \\\"vector\\\" | \\\"scalar\\\" | \\\"string\\\",\\n100:   \\\"result\\\": <value>\\n101: }\\n102: ```\\n103: \\n104: `<value>` refers to the query result data, which has varying formats\\n105: depending on the `resultType`. See the [expression query result\\n106: formats](#expression-query-result-formats).\\n107: \\n108: The following example evaluates the expression `up` at the time\\n109: `2015-07-01T20:10:51.781Z`:\\n110: \\n111: ```bash\\n112: curl 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=up&time=2015-07-01T20:10:51.781Z'\\n113: ```\\n114: \\n115: ```json\\n116: {\\n117:    \\\"status\\\" : \\\"success\\\",\\n118:    \\\"data\\\" : {\\n119:       \\\"resultType\\\" : \\\"vector\\\",\\n120:       \\\"result\\\" : [\\n121:          {\\n122:             \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n123:                \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n124:                \\\"job\\\" : \\\"prometheus\\\",\\n125:                \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9090\\\"\\n126:             },\\n127:             \\\"value\\\": [ 1435781451.781, \\\"1\\\" ]\\n128:          },\\n129:          {\\n130:             \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n131:                \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n132:                \\\"job\\\" : \\\"node\\\",\\n133:                \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9100\\\"\\n134:             },\\n135:             \\\"value\\\" : [ 1435781451.781, \\\"0\\\" ]\\n136:          }\\n137:       ]\\n138:    }\\n139: }\\n140: ```\\n141: \\n142: ### Range queries\\n143: \\n144: The following endpoint evaluates an expression query over a range of time:\\n145: \\n146: ```\\n147: GET /api/v1/query_range\\n148: POST /api/v1/query_range\\n149: ```\\n150: \\n151: URL query parameters:\\n152: \\n153: - `query=<string>`: Prometheus expression query string.\\n154: - `start=<rfc3339 | unix_timestamp>`: Start timestamp, inclusive.\\n155: - `end=<rfc3339 | unix_timestamp>`: End timestamp, inclusive.\\n156: - `step=<duration | float>`: Query resolution step width in `duration` format or float number of seconds.\\n157: - `timeout=<duration>`: Evaluation timeout. Optional. Defaults to and\\n158:    is capped by the value of the `-query.timeout` flag.\\n159: - `limit=<number>`: Maximum number of returned series. Optional. 0 means disabled.\\n160: \\n161: You can URL-encode these parameters directly in the request body by using the `POST` method and\\n162: `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header. This is useful when specifying a large\\n163: query that may breach server-side URL character limits.\\n164: \\n165: The `data` section of the query result has the following format:\\n166: \\n167: ```json\\n168: {\\n169:   \\\"resultType\\\": \\\"matrix\\\",\\n170:   \\\"result\\\": <value>\\n171: }\\n172: ```\\n173: \\n174: For the format of the `<value>` placeholder, see the [range-vector result\\n175: format](#range-vectors).\\n176: \\n177: The following example evaluates the expression `up` over a 30-second range with\\n178: a query resolution of 15 seconds.\\n179: \\n180: ```bash\\n181: curl 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query_range?query=up&start=2015-07-01T20:10:30.781Z&end=2015-07-01T20:11:00.781Z&step=15s'\\n182: ```\\n183: \\n184: ```json\\n185: {\\n186:    \\\"status\\\" : \\\"success\\\",\\n187:    \\\"data\\\" : {\\n188:       \\\"resultType\\\" : \\\"matrix\\\",\\n189:       \\\"result\\\" : [\\n190:          {\\n191:             \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n192:                \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n193:                \\\"job\\\" : \\\"prometheus\\\",\\n194:                \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9090\\\"\\n195:             },\\n196:             \\\"values\\\" : [\\n197:                [ 1435781430.781, \\\"1\\\" ],\\n198:                [ 1435781445.781, \\\"1\\\" ],\\n199:                [ 1435781460.781, \\\"1\\\" ]\\n200:             ]\\n201:          },\\n202:          {\\n203:             \\\"metric\\\" : {\\n204:                \\\"__name__\\\" : \\\"up\\\",\\n205:                \\\"job\\\" : \\\"node\\\",\\n206:                \\\"instance\\\" : \\\"localhost:9091\\\"\\n207:             },\\n208:             \\\"values\\\" : [\\n209:                [ 1435781430.781, \\\"0\\\" ],\\n210:                [ 1435781445.781, \\\"0\\\" ],\\n211:                [ 1435781460.781, \\\"1\\\" ]\\n212:             ]\\n213:          }\\n214:       ]\\n215:    }\\n216: }\\n217: ```\\n218: \\n219: ## Formatting query expressions\\n220: \\n221: The following endpoint formats a PromQL expression in a prettified way:\\n222: \\n223: ```\\n224: GET /api/v1/format_query\\n225: POST /api/v1/format_query\\n226: ```\\n227: \\n228: URL query parameters:\\n229: \\n230: - `query=<string>`: Prometheus expression query string.\\n231: \\n232: You can URL-encode these parameters directly in the request body by using the `POST` method and\\n233: `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header. This is useful when specifying a large\\n234: query that may breach server-side URL character limits.\\n235: \\n236: The `data` section of the query result is a string containing the formatted query expression. Note that any comments are removed in the formatted string.\\n237: \\n238: The following example formats the expression `foo/bar`:\\n239: \\n240: ```bash\\n241: curl 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/format_query?query=foo/bar'\\n242: ```\\n243: \\n244: ```json\\n245: {\\n246:    \\\"status\\\" : \\\"success\\\",\\n247:    \\\"data\\\" : \\\"foo / bar\\\"\\n248: }\\n249: ```\\n250: \\n251: ## Parsing a PromQL expressions into a abstract syntax tree (AST)\\n252: \\n253: This endpoint is **experimental** and might change in the future. It is currently only meant to be used by Prometheus' own web UI, and the endpoint name and exact format returned may change from one Prometheus version to another. It may also be removed again in case it is no longer needed by the UI.\\n254: \\n255: The following endpoint parses a PromQL expression and returns it as a JSON-formatted AST (abstract syntax tree) representation:\\n256: \\n257: ```\\n258: GET /api/v1/parse_query\\n259: POST /api/v1/parse_query\\n260: ```\\n261: \\n262: URL query parameters:\\n263: \\n264: - `query=<string>`: Prometheus expression query string.\\n265: \\n266: You can URL-encode these parameters directly in the request body by using the `POST` method and\\n267: `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` header. This is useful when specifying a large\\n268: query that may breach server-side URL character limits.\\n269: \\n270: The `data` section of the query result is a string containing the AST of the parsed query expression.\\n271: \\n272: The following example parses the expression `foo/bar`:\\n273: \\n274: ```bash\\n275: curl 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/parse_query?query=foo/bar'\\n276: ```\\n277: \\n278: ```json\\n279: {\\n280:    \\\"data\\\" : {\\n281:       \\\"bool\\\" : false,\\n282:       \\\"lhs\\\" : {\\n283:          \\\"matchers\\\" : [\\n284:             {\\n285:                \\\"name\\\" : \\\"__name__\\\",\\n286:                \\\"type\\\" : \\\"=\\\",\\n287:                \\\"value\\\" : \\\"foo\\\"\\n288:             }\\n289:          ],\\n290:          \\\"name\\\" : \\\"foo\\\",\\n291:          \\\"offset\\\" : 0,\\n292:          \\\"startOrEnd\\\" : null,\\n293:          \\\"timestamp\\\" : null,\\n294:          \\\"type\\\" : \\\"vectorSelector\\\"\\n295:       },\\n296:       \\\"matching\\\" : {\\n297:          \\\"card\\\" : \\\"one-to-one\\\",\\n298:          \\\"include\\\" : [],\\n299:          \\\"labels\\\" : [],\\n300:          \\\"on\\\" : false\\n301: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4@ba16cf5a7437eb3b6b71b74557b7a67e1975ad0f/-/blob/docs/querying/api.md%3FL1-300\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01G8YHsd6ELSiwNPyVzkwQW1","session_id":"f81fb71c-8c95-4f5a-a960-5e8bc02395af","uuid":"7dbd6cdf-688d-49eb-87ba-2994a1d9f5fd"}

override status code for different error types.\n// Return false to fall back to default status code.\ntype OverrideErrorCode func(errorNum, error) (code int, override bool)\n\nvar LocalhostRepresentations = []string{\"127.0.0.1\", \"localhost\", \"::1\"}\n\ntype apiError struct {\n\ttyp errorType\n\terr error\n}\n\nfunc (e *apiError) Error() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", e.typ.str, e.err)\n}\n\n// ScrapePoolsRetriever provide the list of all scrape pools.\ntype ScrapePoolsRetriever interface {\n\tScrapePools() []string\n}\n\n// TargetRetriever provides the list stats.NewQueryStats(s)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PrometheusVersion contains build information about Prometheus.\ntype PrometheusVersion struct {\n\tVersion   string `json:\"version\"`\n\tRevision precedence over codecs installed later when evaluating wildcards in Accept headers.\n// The first installed codec is used as a fallback when the Accept header cannot be satisfied or if there is no Accept header.\nfunc (api *API) InstallCodec(codec Codec) {\n\tapi.codecs = append(api.codecs, codec)\n}\n\n// ClearCodecs removes all available codecs from this API, including the default codec installed by NewAPI.\nfunc (api *API) ClearCodecs() {\n\tapi.codecs = nil\n}\n\nfunc setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(r apiFuncResult) apiFuncResult {\n\tif r.err != nil && errors.Is(r.err.err, tsdb.ErrNotReady) {\n\t\tr.err.typ = errorUnavailable\n\t}\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\nfunc (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\n\twrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\t\thf := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\t\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n\t\t\tresult := setUnavailStatusOnTSDBNotReady(f(r))\n\t\t\tif result.finalizer != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdefer result.finalizer()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif result.err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapi.respondError(w, result.err, result.data)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.data != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapi.respond(w, r, result.data, result.warnings, r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn api.ready(httputil.CompressionHandler{\n\t\t\tHandler: hf,\n\t\t}.ServeHTTP)\n\t}\n\n\twrapAgent := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\t\treturn wrap(func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\t\t\tif api.isAgent {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, errors.New(\"unavailable with Prometheus Agent\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn f(r)\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tr.Options(\"/*path\", wrap(api.options))\n\n\tr.Get(\"/query\", parser.Value     `json:\"result\"`\n\tStats      stats.QueryStats `json:\"stats,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc invalidParamError(err error, parameter string) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{\n\t\terrorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid parameter %q: %w\", parameter, err),\n\t}, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) options(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"time\", api.now())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"time\")\n\t}\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), ts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) formatQuery(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{expr.Pretty(0), nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) parseQuery(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: translateAST(expr), err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc extractQueryOpts(r *http.Request) (promql.QueryOpts, error) {\n\tvar duration time.Duration\n\n\tif strDuration := r.FormValue(\"lookback_delta\"); strDuration != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedDuration, err := parseDuration(strDuration)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing lookback delta duration: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tduration = parsedDuration\n\t}\n\n\treturn promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(r.FormValue(\"stats\") == \"all\", duration), nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryRange(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tstart, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"start\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"end\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start time\"), \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\tif step <= 0 {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n\t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n\tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), start, end, step)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start timestamp\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\n\tif len(selectors) < 1 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n\tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n\tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n\tswitch {\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n\t}\n\n\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\thints := &storage.LabelHints{\n\t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tdefer q.Close()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tnames    []string\n\t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t)\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\n\t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err := q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n\t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n\t\t\t\tlabelNamesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Convert the map to an array.\n\t\tnames = make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\n\t\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\n\t\t\tnames = append(names, key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(names)\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n\t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tnames, warnings, err = q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif names == nil {\n\t\tnames = []string{}\n\t}\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(names) > limit {\n\t\tnames = names[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{names, nil, warnings, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tname := route.Param(ctx, \"name\")\n\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(name, \"U__\") {\n\t\tname = model.UnescapeName(name, model.ValueEncodingEscaping)\n\t}\n\n\tif !model.UTF8Validation.IsValidLabelName(name) {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid label name: %q\", name)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\thints := &storage.LabelHints{\n\t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\n\t// in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tq.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tcloser := func() {\n\t\tq.Close()\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tvals     []string\n\t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t)\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar callWarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t\tlabelValuesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n\t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n\t\t\t\tlabelValuesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvals = make([]string, 0, len(labelValuesSet))\n\t\tfor val := range labelValuesSet {\n\t\t\tvals = append(vals, val)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n\t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tvals, warnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif vals == nil {\n\t\t\tvals = []string{}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tslices.Sort(vals)\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(vals) > limit {\n\t\tvals = vals[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{vals, nil, warnings, closer}\n}\n\nvar (\n\t// MinTime is the default timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\n\n\t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\n\n\tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n)\n\nfunc (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\n\t// in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tq.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tcloser := func() {\n\t\tq.Close()\n\t}\n\n\thints := &storage.SelectHints{\n\t\tStart: timestamp.FromTime(start),\n\t\tEnd:   timestamp.FromTime(end),\n\t\tFunc:  \"series\", // There is no series function, this token is used for lookups that don't need samples.\n\t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\tvar set storage.SeriesSet\n\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar sets []storage.SeriesSet\n\t\tfor _, mset := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\t// We need to sort this select results to merge (deduplicate) the series sets later.\n\t\t\ts := q.Select(ctx, true, hints, mset...)\n\t\t\tsets = append(sets, s)\n\t\t}\n\t\tset = storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(sets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// At this point at least one match exists.\n\t\tset = q.Select(ctx, false, hints, matcherSets[0]...)\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics := []labels.Labels{}\n\n\twarnings := set.Warnings()\n\n\ti := 1\n\tfor set.Next() {\n\t\tif i%checkContextEveryNIterations == 0 {\n\t\t\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, closer}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\ti++\n\n\t\tmetrics = append(metrics, set.At().Labels())\n\n\t\tif limit > 0 && len(metrics) > limit {\n\t\t\tmetrics = metrics[:limit]\n\t\t\twarnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif set.Err() != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(set.Err()), warnings, closer}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) dropSeries(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"not implemented\")}, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// Target has the information for one target.\ntype Target struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\t// Any labels that are added to this target and its metrics.\n\tLabels labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n\n\tScrapePool string `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n\tScrapeURL  string `json:\"scrapeUrl\"`\n\tGlobalURL  string `json:\"globalUrl\"`\n\n\tLastError          string              `json:\"lastError\"`\n\tLastScrape         time.Time           `json:\"lastScrape\"`\n\tLastScrapeDuration float64             `json:\"lastScrapeDuration\"`\n\tHealth             scrape.TargetHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\n\tScrapeInterval string `json:\"scrapeInterval\"`\n\tScrapeTimeout  string `json:\"scrapeTimeout\"`\n}\n\ntype ScrapePoolsDiscovery struct {\n\tScrapePools []string `json:\"scrapePools\"`\n}\n\n// DroppedTarget has the information for one target that was dropped during relabelling.\ntype DroppedTarget struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\tScrapePool       string        `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n}\n\n// TargetDiscovery has all the active targets.\ntype TargetDiscovery struct {\n\tActiveTargets       []*Target        `json:\"activeTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargets      []*DroppedTarget `json:\"droppedTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargetCounts map[string]int   `json:\"droppedTargetCounts\"`\n}\n\n// GlobalURLOptions contains fields used for deriving the global URL for local targets.\ntype GlobalURLOptions struct {\n\tListenAddress string\n\tHost          string\n\tScheme        string\n}\n\n// sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n// Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n// original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n// brackets.\nfunc sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n\thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n\tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n\t\tvar errWithPort error\n\t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n\t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n\t\t\terr = nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn host, port, err\n}\n\nfunc getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n\thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn u, err\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n\t\tif host == lhr {\n\t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n\t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n\t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n\t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n\t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n\t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n\t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n\t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n\t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n\t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n\t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n\t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n\t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = host\n\t\t\t\tif port != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn u, nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tnames := api.scrapePoolsRetriever(r.Context()).ScrapePools()\n\tsort.Strings(names)\n\tres := &ScrapePoolsDiscovery{ScrapePools: names}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: res, err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targets(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tgetSortedPools := func(targets map[string][]*scrape.Target) ([]string, int) {\n\t\tvar n int\n\t\tpools := make([]string, 0, len(targets))\n\t\tfor p, t := range targets {\n\t\t\tpools = append(pools, p)\n\t\t\tn += len(t)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(pools)\n\t\treturn pools, n\n\t}\n\n\tscrapePool := r.URL.Query().Get(\"scrapePool\")\n\tstate := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"state\"))\n\tshowActive := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"active\"\n\tshowDropped := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"dropped\"\n\tres := &TargetDiscovery{}\n\tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n\n\tif showActive {\n\t\ttargetsActive := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive()\n\t\tactivePools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsActive)\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = make([]*Target, 0, numTargets)\n\n\t\tfor _, pool := range activePools {\n\t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsActive[pool] {\n\t\t\t\tlastErrStr := \"\"\n\t\t\t\tlastErr := target.LastError()\n\t\t\t\tif lastErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlastErrStr = lastErr.Error()\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tglobalURL, err := getGlobalURL(target.URL(), api.globalURLOptions)\n\n\t\t\t\tres.ActiveTargets = append(res.ActiveTargets, &Target{\n\t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:           target.Labels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeURL:        target.URL().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tGlobalURL:        globalURL.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError: func() string {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err == nil && lastErrStr == \"\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err != nil:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", lastErrStr, err).Error()\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn lastErrStr\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrape:         target.LastScrape(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrapeDuration: target.LastScrapeDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:             target.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeInterval:     target.GetValue(model.ScrapeIntervalLabel),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeTimeout:      target.GetValue(model.ScrapeTimeoutLabel),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = []*Target{}\n\t}\n\tif showDropped {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargetCounts = api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDroppedCounts()\n\n\t\ttargetsDropped := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDropped()\n\t\tdroppedPools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsDropped)\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = make([]*DroppedTarget, 0, numTargets)\n\t\tfor _, pool := {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = []*DroppedTarget{}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc matchLabels(lset labels.Labels, matchers []*labels.Matcher) bool {\n\tfor _, m := range matchers {\n\t\tif !m.Matches(lset.Get(m.Name)) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targetMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := {\n\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n\t\t\t\t\tTarget: targetLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:   md.Type,\n\t\t\t\t\tHelp:   md.Help,\n\t\t\t\t\tUnit: `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) alerts(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\talertingRules := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).AlertingRules()\n\talerts := []*Alert{}\n\n\tfor _, alertingRule := apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rulesAlerts []*rules.Alert) []*Alert {\n\tapiAlerts := make([]*Alert, len(rulesAlerts))\n\tfor i, ruleAlert := range rulesAlerts {\n\t\tapiAlerts[i] = &Alert{\n\t\t\tLabels:      ruleAlert.Labels,\n\t\t\tAnnotations: ruleAlert.Annotations,\n\t\t\tState:       ruleAlert.State.String(),\n\t\t\tActiveAt:    &ruleAlert.ActiveAt,\n\t\t\tValue:       strconv.FormatFloat(ruleAlert.Value, 'e', -1, 64),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tapiAlerts[i].KeepFiringSince = &ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiAlerts\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) metricMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tmetrics := map[string]map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tlimitPerMetric := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit_per_metric\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limitPerMetric, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit_per_metric must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, mm := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: mm.Type, Help: mm.Help, Unit: mm.Unit}\n\t\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[mm.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[mm.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: md.Type, Help: md.Help, Unit: md.Unit}\n\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[md.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n\tres := map[string][]metadata.Metadata{}\n\tfor name, set := range metrics {\n\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts := []metadata.Metadata{}\n\t\tfor metadata := range set {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n\t\t}\n\t\tres[name] = s\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\ntype RuleDiscovery struct {\n\tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n\tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\ntype RuleGroup struct {\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\tFile string `json:\"file\"`\n\t// In order to preserve rule ordering, while exposing type (alerting or recording)\n\t// specific properties, both alerting and recording rules are exposed in the\n\t// same array.\n\tRules          []Rule    `json:\"rules\"`\n\tInterval       float64   `json:\"interval\"`\n\tLimit          int       `json:\"limit\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64   `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n}\n\ntype Rule any\n\ntype AlertingRule struct {\n\t// State can be \"pending\", \"firing\", \"inactive\".\n\tState          string           `json:\"state\"`\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tDuration       float64          `json:\"duration\"`\n\tKeepFiringFor  float64          `json:\"keepFiringFor\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations    labels.Labels    `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tAlerts         []*Alert         `json:\"alerts\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of an alertingRule is always \"alerting\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\ntype RecordingRule struct {\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of a recordingRule is always \"recording\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) rules(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n\t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn set\n\t}\n\n\trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n\trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n\tfSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"file[]\"])\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\truleGroups := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).RuleGroups()\n\tres := &RuleDiscovery{RuleGroups: make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))}\n\ttyp := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n\n\tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n\t}\n\n\treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n\treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n\t}\n\n\tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n\tif parseErr != nil {\n\t\treturn *parseErr\n\t}\n\n\trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n\n\tfoundToken := false\n\n\tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfoundToken = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tapiRuleGroup := &RuleGroup{\n\t\t\tName:           grp.Name(),\n\t\t\tFile:           grp.File(),\n\t\t\tInterval:       grp.Interval().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLimit:          grp.Limit(),\n\t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n\t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n\t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n\n\t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n\t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts []*Alert\n\t\t\t\tif !excludeAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tactiveAlerts = rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rule.ActiveAlerts())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = AlertingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tState:          rule.State().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tDuration:       rule.HoldDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tKeepFiringFor:  rule.KeepFiringFor().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAnnotations:    rule.Annotations(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"alerting\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcase *rules.RecordingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnRecording {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = RecordingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n\t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n\t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n\t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n\t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trgs = append(rgs, apiRuleGroup)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"invalid group_next_token '%v'. were rule groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n\t}\n\n\tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n\texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n\n\tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting exclude_alerts: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn excludeAlerts, nil\n}\n\nfunc parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r *http.Request) (int64, string, *apiFuncResult) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups int64 = -1\n\t\terr             error\n\t)\n\tmaxGroups := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_limit\")\n\tnextToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_next_token\")\n\n\tif nextToken != \"\" && maxGroups == \"\" {\n\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be present in order to paginate over the groups\"), \"group_next_token\")\n\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups, err = strconv.ParseInt(maxGroups, 10, 32)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"group_limit needs to be a valid number: %w\", err), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parsedMaxGroups <= 0 {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be greater than 0\"), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif parsedMaxGroups > 0 {\n\t\treturn parsedMaxGroups, nextToken, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn -1, \"\", nil\n}\n\nfunc getRuleGroupNextToken(file, group string) string {\n\th := sha1.New()\n\th.Write([]byte(file + \";\" + group))\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n}\n\ntype prometheusConfig struct {\n\tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstatus, err := api.runtimeInfo()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{status, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{status, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) apiFuncResult{api.flagsMap, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// TSDBStat holds the information about individual cardinality.\ntype TSDBStat struct {\n\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n\tValue uint64 `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\n// HeadStats has information about the TSDB head.\ntype HeadStats struct {\n\tNumSeries     uint64 `json:\"numSeries\"`\n\tNumLabelPairs int    `json:\"numLabelPairs\"`\n\tChunkCount    int64  `json:\"chunkCount\"`\n\tMinTime       int64  `json:\"minTime\"`\n\tMaxTime       int64  `json:\"maxTime\"`\n}\n\n// TSDBStatus has information of slice.\nfunc TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(stats []index.Stat) []TSDBStat {\n\tresult := make([]TSDBStat, 0, len(stats))\n\tfor _, item := range stats {\n\t\titem := TSDBStat{Name: item.Name, Value: item.Count}\n\t\tresult = append(result, item)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveTSDBBlocks(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tblockMetas, err := api.db.BlockMetas()\n\tif err != nil (api *API) notificationsSSE(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\thttputil.SetCORS(w, api.CORSOrigin, r)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"keep-alive\")\n\n\t// Subscribe to notifications.\n\tnotifications, unsubscribe, ok := api.notificationsSub()\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer unsubscribe()\n\n\t// Set up a flusher to push the response to the client.\n\tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Streaming unsupported\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Flush the response to ensure the headers are immediately and eventSource\n\t// onopen is triggered client-side.\n\tflusher.Flush()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase notification := <-notifications:\n\t\t\t// Marshal the notification to JSON.\n\t\t\tjsonData, err := json.Marshal(notification)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Write the event data in SSE format with JSON content.\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"data: %s\\n\\n\", jsonData)\n\n\t\t\t// Flush the response to ensure the data is sent immediately.\n\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n\t\tcase <-r.Context().Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) remoteRead(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// This is only really for tests - this will never be nil IRL.\n\tif api.remoteReadHandler != nil {\n\t\tapi.remoteReadHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t} else {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"not found\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) remoteWrite(w with --web.enable-remote-write-receiver\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) otlpWrite(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif api.otlpWriteHandler != nil {\n\t\tapi.otlpWriteHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n\t} else {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"otlp write receiver needs to be enabled with --web.enable-otlp-receiver\", http.StatusNotFound)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif !api.enableAdmin {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, s := range r.Form[\"match[]\"] {\n\t\tmatchers, err := parser.ParseMetricSelector(s)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := api.db.Delete(r.Context(), timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), matchers...); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) snapshot(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif !api.enableAdmin {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tvar (\n\t\tskipHead bool\n\t\terr      error\n\t)\n\tif r.FormValue(\"skip_head\") != \"\" {\n\t\tskipHead, err = strconv.ParseBool(r.FormValue(\"skip_head\"))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn := w.Write(b); err != nil {\n\t\tapi.logger.Error(\"error writing response\", \"url\", req.URL, \"bytesWritten\", n, \"err\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) negotiateCodec(req *http.Request, resp *Response) (Codec, error) {\n\tfor _, clause := range goautoneg.ParseAccept(req.Header.Get(\"Accept\")) {\n\t\tfor _, codec := range api.codecs {\n\t\t\tif codec.ContentType().Satisfies(clause) && It overflows int64\", s)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\n\t}\n\tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\n\t\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\n\t}\n\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration\", s)\n}\n\nfunc parseMatchersParam(matchers []string) ([][]*labels.Matcher, error) {\n\tmatcherSets, err := parser.ParseMetricSelectors(matchers)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\nOUTER:\n\tfor _, ms := range matcherSets {\n\t\tfor _, lm := range ms {\n\t\t\tif lm != nil && !lm.Matches(\"\") {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue OUTER\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"match[] must contain at least one non-empty matcher\")\n\t}\n\treturn matcherSets, nil\n}\n\n// parseLimitParam returning 0 means no limit is to be applied.\nfunc parseLimitParam(limitStr string) (limit int, err error) {\n\tif limitStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn limit, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err = strconv.Atoi(limitStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn limit, err\n\t}\n\tif limit < 0 {\n\t\treturn limit, errors.New(\"limit must be non-negative\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn limit, nil\n}\n\n// toHintLimit increases the API limit, as returned by parseLimitParam, by 1.\n// This allows for emitting warnings when the results are truncated.\nfunc toHintLimit(limit int) int {\n\t// 0 means no limit and avoid int overflow\n\tif limit > 0 && limit < math.MaxInt {\n\t\treturn limit + 1\n\t}\n\treturn limit\n}\n\n// truncateResults truncates result for queryRange() and query().\n// No truncation for other types(Scalars or Strings).\nfunc truncateResults(result *promql.Result, limit int) (*promql.Result, bool) {\n\tisTruncated := false\n\n\tswitch v := result.Value.(type) {\n\tcase promql.Matrix:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\tcase promql.Vector:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return the modified result. `SilenceMatcher` - Matcher object for silence rules\n     - `SilenceRequest` - Single silence creation request\n     - `BulkSilenceRequest` - Array of silence creation requests\n     - `BulkSilenceResponse` - Response with array of created silence IDs\n     - `BulkSilenceError` - Error response with index information\n   \n   - Implements `silencesBulk()` handler that:\n     - Parses JSON request body\n     - Validates empty array check\n     - Validates all silences before creating any (atomic semantics)\n     - Returns array of IDs on success or detailed error with failing index\n   \n   - Includes validation for:\n     - Non-empty matchers list\n     - Valid matcher names\n     - Valid time ranges (endsAt > startsAt)\n     - Required `createdBy` field\n\n2. **`web/api/v1/silences_bulk_test.go`** - New test file with comprehensive test coverage\n   - `TestBulkSilenceCreation()` - Tests for the main handler:\n     - Successful bulk creation with multiple silences\n     - Validation failure on empty matchers\n     - Validation failure on invalid matcher (empty name)\n     - Empty array handling\n     - Partial validation failure (error at specific index)\n     - Missing `createdBy` field validation\n     - Invalid time range validation\n     - Invalid JSON body handling\n   \n   - `TestBulkSilenceIntegration()` - Integration test with HTTP handler\n\n3. **`web/api/v1/api.go`** - Modified to register the new endpoint\n   - Added route: `r.Post(\"/silences/bulk\", wrap(api.silencesBulk))`\n   - Placed in the \"Silences APIs\" section after alerts/rules endpoints\n\n### API Endpoint Specification\n\n**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/silences/bulk`\n\n**Request Format:**\n```json\n{\n  \"silences\": [\n    {\n      \"matchers\": [\n        {\"name\": \"alertname\", \"value\": \"TestAlert\", \"isRegex\": false}\n      ],\n      \"startsAt\": \"2024-03-01T12:00:00Z\",\n      \"endsAt\": \"2024-03-01T13:00:00Z\",\n      \"createdBy\": \"operator\",\n      \"comment\": \"Maintenance window\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Success Response (HTTP 200):**\n```json\n{\n  \"status\": \"success\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"ids\": [\"1709308800000000000\", \"1709308801000000000\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response (HTTP 400):**\n```json\n{\n  \"status\": \"error\",\n  \"errorType\": \"bad_data\",\n  \"error\": \"validation error at index 0: matchers cannot be empty\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"index\": 0,\n    \"message\": \"matchers cannot be empty\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Key Features\n\n✅ **Atomic Validation** - All silences validated before creating any  \n✅ **Error Reporting** - Index of failing silence included in error response  \n✅ **Follows Prometheus Patterns** - Uses same error handling, response format, and handler patterns as existing API  \n✅ **Comprehensive Tests** -
